title: podman --remote is not compatible with docker.socket
body:
### Issue Description

I tried to run some containers via podman-remote using docker as backend by setting `CONTAINER_HOST=unix://var/run/docker.socket` - I thought that because podman is meant to be compatible with docker that this should work.

However what I observed is that podman will error out with this error:

```
podman --remote ps --log-level debug
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --remote ps --log-level debug) 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.6.1/libpod/_ping 
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: ping response was 404
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines 
```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install `moby-engine` and enable/start `docker.socket`
2. set `export CONTAINER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock`
3. run `podman --remote ps`


### Describe the results you received

I get an error:

```
podman --remote ps --log-level debug
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --remote ps --log-level debug) 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.6.1/libpod/_ping 
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: ping response was 404
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines 
```

I believe this is because the `_ping` endpoint is actually not versioned. There is a previous issue here: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/6548

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect that `podman --remote` behaves the same when using a docker.socket as backend vs when using podman.socket as backend.

This is what happens when I do that.

```
export CONTAINER_HOST=unix:///var/run/podman/podman.sock
podman --remote ps --log-level debug
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --remote ps --log-level debug) 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.6.1/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.6.1/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.6.1/libpod/containers/json 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPostRunE(podman --remote ps --log-level debug) 
DEBU[0000] Shutting down engines  
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

CONTAINER_HOST=unix://var/run/podman/podman.sock - this shows correct behaviour
CONTAINER_HOST=unix://var/run/docker.sock - this shows incorrect behaviour

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @akdev1l 

> I thought that because podman is meant to be compatible with docker that this should work.

Podman is compatible with Docker but I think you approach it from a different angle.  You can run Docker against the Podman Socket but you cannot run it the other way around.  Podman is many features and functionalities that Docker does not support, so the Podman (remote) client makes use of the Podman-specific REST API.
